cd 0.1.1
  • Reference
    • Climate Departure Analysis for a Watershed
  • Changelog

cd

Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis

Overview

cd computes climate anomalies from ERA5-Land reanalysis data for custom areas of interest. It downloads raw climate data, derives variables (VPD, RH, soil moisture), publishes Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs to a STAC catalog, and provides consumer functions to extract zonal statistics, compute baselines and anomalies for arbitrary reference periods, and run trend analysis.

Installation 

pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/cd")

Quick start 

library(cd)

# Load the live STAC catalog and an example area of interest
catalog <- cd_catalog()
aoi <- sf::st_read(
  system.file("extdata", "example_aoi_kotl.gpkg", package = "cd"),
  quiet = TRUE
)

# Extract zonal mean time series
ts <- cd_extract(catalog, aoi)

# Compute baseline and anomalies
bl <- cd_baseline(ts, baseline_years = 1951:1955)
ano <- cd_anomaly(ts, bl)

# Trend analysis
trn <- cd_trend(ano, trend_start = 1951)

# Reporting table
cd_summary(trn)

# Compare time windows directly
cd_compare(ts, window_a = 1956:1960, window_b = 1951:1955)
